title: Source Configuration v1.0
description: Validates a YAML-driven source configuration file consumed by the critical-infrastructure ingestion engine at pipeline startup.

schemaVersion.description: Version of this schema specification the config file conforms to; the runtime validator rejects configs whose version it does not recognise.

metadata.description: Identity and classification of the data source, written to every audit log entry and the lineage catalog.

sourceId.description: Machine-readable unique identifier for this source used as the primary key in the lineage catalog (lowercase, hyphens only).

sourceName.description: Human-readable display name shown in dashboards and alerts.

sector.description: Regulated sector this source belongs to.

owner.description: Team or individual accountable for this source, used for alert routing.

environment.description: Deployment environment; controls default retention policies and alert severity.

tags.description: Arbitrary labels for grouping sources in dashboards.

connection.description: Connectivity details for the source system; conditional fields are enforced based on the value of the type property.

type.description: Source system type that determines which connector class the engine loads.

credentialsRef.description: Path to credentials in the external secrets manager (Vault or AWS KMS); never a literal credential.

host.description: Hostname or IP of the source server; required when type is jdbc or api.

port.description: Port number of the source server; required when type is jdbc or api.

database.description: Database or catalog name on the source server; required when type is jdbc.

jdbcDriver.description: JDBC driver to load; required when type is jdbc.

filePath.description: Absolute path or URI to the source file or directory; required when type is file.

fileFormat.description: File format to parse; required when type is file.

kafkaTopic.description: Kafka topic name to consume from; required when type is kafka.

kafkaBootstrapServers.description: Comma-separated list of Kafka broker addresses; required when type is kafka.

ingestion.description: Controls the extraction strategy, parallelism, scheduling, and timeout for a pipeline run.

mode.description: Extraction strategy: full loads all available data; incremental loads only records added or modified since the last watermark.

incrementalColumn.description: Column used to determine new or changed records; required when mode is incremental and must be a monotonically increasing timestamp or integer.

watermarkStorage.description: URI where the engine persists the last-seen watermark value between runs; required when mode is incremental.

batchSize.description: Number of rows fetched per JDBC round-trip or file chunk.

parallelism.description: Number of concurrent Spark tasks for extraction.

schedule.description: Cron expression defining when the pipeline runs automatically; if absent the pipeline is triggered externally by the orchestrator.

timeout.description: Maximum wall-clock seconds the engine will wait for extraction to complete before aborting and raising an alert.

schemaEnforcement.description: Controls whether extracted records are validated against a declared schema before being written to storage.

enabled.description: If false, schema validation is skipped and all records proceed to storage regardless of structure.

mode.description: Enforcement strictness; required when enabled is true: strict rejects any record with an unexpected column or type mismatch, permissive routes violating records to quarantine.

registryRef.description: Path to the JSON Schema document in the schema registry; required when enabled is true and fetched by the engine at pipeline startup.

qualityRules.description: Reserved for Phase 2 (v0.2.0); in v0.1.0 the only valid configuration is enabled: false and no other fields are accepted.

enabled.description: Must be false in v0.1.0; setting to true will cause a validator error until the v0.2.0 engine is deployed.

quarantine.description: Defines where failed records are written and how errors are classified so that no record is ever silently dropped.

enabled.description: If false, failed records are discarded rather than quarantined; only acceptable in development environments with explicit justification.

path.description: URI of the quarantine storage location; required when enabled is true and must differ from the Bronze storage path.

retentionDays.description: Number of days quarantined records are retained before deletion.

errorClassification.description: If true, each quarantined record is tagged with a structured error code identifying which validation rule it failed.

storage.description: Defines where and how successfully validated records are written to the lakehouse Bronze layer.

layer.description: Target lakehouse layer; in v0.1.0 only bronze is supported by the ingestion engine.

format.description: Storage format providing ACID transactions, time travel, and schema evolution required for audit compliance.

path.description: URI of the target storage location.

partitionBy.description: List of column names used to partition the output dataset.

compactionEnabled.description: If true, the engine runs a compaction step after each write to merge small files.

monitoring.description: Controls the Prometheus metrics endpoint and SLA alerting emitted during a pipeline run.

metricsEnabled.description: If true, the engine exposes a Prometheus metrics endpoint on prometheusPort for the duration of the pipeline run.

prometheusPort.description: Port on which the Prometheus metrics endpoint is exposed; required when metricsEnabled is true.

slaThresholdSeconds.description: Maximum acceptable wall-clock duration for a complete pipeline run in seconds; exceeding this value emits an SLA breach event.

alertOnFailure.description: If true, the engine emits an alert when the pipeline terminates with a failure status.

audit.description: Controls the audit trail written to the lineage catalog per pipeline run, directly supporting Dodd-Frank, NERC CIP, and NIST CSF compliance requirements.

enabled.description: If false, no audit records are written; must be true in staging and prod environments.

lineageTracking.description: If true, the engine records source-to-target lineage for every pipeline run including source system, extraction timestamp, record counts, target path, and schema version.

immutableRawEnabled.description: If true, the engine enforces that the Bronze layer path is write-once with no overwrites or deletes permitted after initial write.

retentionDays.description: Number of days audit records are retained; default of 2555 days (7 years) matches common U.S. financial services retention requirements.
